dialogueChunks.intro: You found signs of them, {player_name}! But the trail just got much more complicated.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: The markers you recovered confirm that {target_npc} was alive recently. But the trail leads straight into {enemy_type} territory {location_hint}. The area is crawling with them. You will need to fight your way through and clear the path before we can follow the trail any further.
dialogueChunks.outro: They're close. I can feel it. But close isn't found, and every wrong turn costs time. The {quest_threat} {quest_threat_is} closing the window we have.

dialogueChunks.intro: {player_name}, we found where {target_npc} has been. And the truth is harder than not knowing.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} didn't vanish by accident. They were captured by the {enemy_type} and put to work in a labor camp {location_hint}. A refugee who escaped described {target_npc} by name. The camp is guarded, fortified, and deep in hostile territory. You will need to fight through the {enemy_type} guarding the camp to break them out.
dialogueChunks.outro: Alive but captive. That's better than the alternatives, but it means the rescue just became ten times harder. Saving the {quest_stakes} depends on moving fast.

dialogueChunks.intro: We found {target_npc}'s shelter, {player_name}. They've been surviving alone this whole time.
dialogueChunks.plotStep: {target_npc} is alive and hiding in a makeshift camp {location_hint}, but they're injured and can't travel. The area is thick with {enemy_type}. We need you to clear the {enemy_type} out of the area so we can get {target_npc} home safely. They say the {enemy_type} move in waves: there's a narrow window between patrols.
dialogueChunks.outro: They survived alone for weeks in the wilderness. The least we can do is get them the last mile home.
